description: A soft white fibrous substance surrounding seeds of the cotton plant; the breath-able cloth fiber. 'Sophae' names what plants give for covering—soft, light, kind to skin.
sound_symbolism: The word softens airy. 'So' touches with gentle softness, fiber yielding. 'Pha' /ɸa/ breathes with airy lightness. Then 'e' opens to comfortable coolness.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'sophae wetha' (cotton cloth). Plant fiber, lighter than 'mophi' (wool). Used for warm-weather clothing.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Renewable fiber—organically grown cotton as sustainable textile source.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cotton clothed civilizations—the plant fiber of light covering, breathable comfort.
